description: Master C++ from standard syntax to advanced Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and the Standard Template Library (STL).

topics.title: Introduction to OOP
topics.definition: Object-Oriented Programming is a paradigm that organizes software design around data, or objects, rather than functions and logic. An object can be defined as a data field that has unique attributes and behavior.
topics.explanation: C++ extends the C language with OOP features. Instead of writing procedural code where functions modify global data, OOP encapsulates data and the functions that manipulate it into 'classes'.
pros: Modularity: Objects are self-contained, making troubleshooting easier.
Code reuse through inheritance and composition.
cons: Steeper learning curve than procedural programming.
Can lead to bloated code if over-engineered.
Slightly heavier memory overhead due to vtables.
syntax: class ClassName { public: data_type member; return_type method() { ... } };

examples.title: Basic Class Structure
examples.description: Defining a class, creating an instance, and invoking a member function.

bestPractices: Keep class data members private and provide public getter/setter methods (Encapsulation).
Use constructors to initialize objects in a valid state.
Favor composition over inheritance when possible.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the semicolon `;` at the end of a class definition.
Making all class variables public out of laziness.
Creating deep and overly complex inheritance hierarchies.
